codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.introduc: The Seven Stars arts are founded on Qi. This codex records progression, important items, spells, and exploration objectives. Stage 0 introduces Qi and the starting spells before the search for Pegasus, Ice Dipper, and Hound Claw begins.

Use the codex to open it at any time. Scroll through the staged directory on the left, select a chapter, and use the arrows below the text to turn pages. Locked chapters require clues found in the world.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.qi.title: Qi: A Second Life
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.qi.page1: Every creature has Qi, with a base maximum of 100. It naturally restores 2% of its maximum every 20 ticks. Most spells spend the caster's Qi and may drain the target's Qi as well.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.qi.page2: Taking a normal hit usually removes another 5–10 Qi. Some armor blocks this ordinary drain, but special guard-breaking attacks may disable that protection. Watch health and Qi together.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.qi.page3: Reaching zero Qi causes Qi Break: the creature takes true damage equal to 34% of maximum health, then regains 50% Qi. Against heavily armored foes, forcing a Qi Break can be the best opening.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.spellcas: Spell scrolls teach their matching art, while the Seven Star Scroll carries and organizes learned powers. Select an art on the skill wheel and cast it with the cast key. Low Qi, cooldowns, or missing equipment can prevent a cast.
Spells usually have two effects: direct physical damage and Qi drain. Armor, remaining Qi, and disabled protection all influence the best choice, so surface damage is not the whole story.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage0.basic_sp: Light Wave is a basic ranged art and an important way to activate star-powered devices such as Soul-Calming Lamps. When a Tormented Wraith cannot be harmed, find a nearby lamp first.
Seven Star is one of the starting arts. Use it to learn the relationship between skill selection, cast rank, and Qi cost. Keep a reserve instead of casting yourself into Qi Break.
Six Meridian Sword shapes Qi into a striking sword force. Confirm range and aim before casting; a missed spell still wastes the resources committed to it.
Golden Finger is another starting art. Rotate it with Light Wave, Seven Star, and Six Meridian Sword according to the situation instead of committing all Qi to one attack.
Stage 1 has three independent paths: Pegasus, Ice Dipper, and Hound Claw. They explore mobility, ranged control, and close pursuit while yielding their own materials, equipment, and spells.
Explore the Abandoned Stable, Frozen Observatory, and Abandoned Kennel. Learn Pegasus Step, Ice Dipper Shot, and Hound Claw; all three are required before Stage 2.
Find an Abandoned Stable and the Star-Mane Pegasus. Gather Pegasus Mane and Star Hoof Fragments, restore a Complete Star Hoof, and watch for the Star Rein Bell and stable loot.
Final objective: obtain and learn Pegasus Step. The Star Hoof Spear favors mobile combat, while Pegasus Step turns movement into part of both offense and defense.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage1.ice_dipp: Enter the Frozen Observatory, defeat Frost-Shell Silverfish, and collect shell fragments, frost powder, and Frost Marrow Crystals. The gauge and wand are key relics of this route.
Final objective: obtain and learn Ice Dipper Shot. It provides a ranged option unlike the Pegasus route. Avoid being driven into Qi Break by repeated attacks in tight rooms.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage1.hound_cl: Explore an Abandoned Kennel and hunt Black-Mane Hounds. Gather Black Mane, Cracked Claw Bone, and Hound Fangs; the collar, dagger, and trophy rack preserve the old hunters' story.
Final objective: obtain and learn Hound Claw. This path emphasizes pressure and pursuit. Confirm Pegasus Step and Ice Dipper Shot are also learned before advancing.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage1.completi: Stage check: Pegasus Step, Ice Dipper Shot, and Hound Claw are all learned. Prepare recovery supplies and dependable gear; the Ancient Goat Temple and Triangle Stone Town turn Qi offense and defense into a real trial.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage2.overview: Stage 2 centers on two suits of armor and a guard-breaking lesson: claim the horn from the Ancient Goat Temple, learn Chop from the hunters in raids, then light the lamps of Triangle Stone Town and find its triangle relics.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage2.goat_hor: Goat Horn armor prevents ordinary hit-based Qi drain while worn. Certain creatures and attacks can still disable it. This protects Qi, not health damage.
Goat Horn Spike requires the horn armor. It fires a line of spikes for high damage and Qi drain with a 50-second cooldown. Save it for armored or nearly broken targets.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage2.goat_tem: Ancient Goat Temples appear in Jagged Peaks. Cultists once hunted star-touched goats and enshrined their horns to contact the stars. Star magic makes the temple difficult to break with ordinary tools.

The temple holds spawners, ancient cultists, and Seven Stars loot. Their spells can cause early Qi Breaks. Advance floor by floor and claim the horn at the summit before crafting its armor and spell scroll.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage2.chop.pag: Goat Hunter Butchers may join village raids. They hunt star-touched goats and use Chop to break horn defenses. Defeat one for a Bloody Cleaver and related materials.

Slay an Ancient Cultist with the Bloody Cleaver to obtain the Chop scroll. Chop costs little Qi and deals modest area damage, but disables Goat Horn armor for 10 seconds—the core counterplay lesson of this stage.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage2.triangle: Triangle Stone Town is a dark underground ruin haunted by Tormented Wraiths. They resist direct harm until Light Wave charges a Soul-Calming Lamp and the wraith is drawn into its light.
A few wraiths occur naturally, and one room hides a spawner. Light lamps as you advance and search the library and hidden chests. Triangle Fragments and the Triangle Scroll lie deep inside.
Triangle is the armor after Goat Horn. While worn, it can cast Seven Scattered Strikes every 10 seconds at very low cost for sustained pressure and Qi drain. Specific attacks can disable it.
Known weaknesses include Light Wave projectiles and Azure Dragon attacks. Gather Triangle Fragments and a core, then use the Triangle Scroll. This completes all Stage 0, 1, and 2 routes.
codex.sevenstars.chapter.stage3.azure_dr: Azure starlight gleams across the scale, and the codex leaves a blank chapter for the Azure Dragon. Its detailed record has not yet been written.
message.sevenstars.armor_disabled: Goat Horn and Triangle armor abilities are temporarily disabled
message.sevenstars.azure_dragon.telegrap: §cAzure Dragon raises its feet: leave the red ring!
§cAzure Dragon coils its tail: leave the rear arc!
§bAzure Dragon is charging its spell breath: watch its head and prepare to dodge sideways!
§cSpell breath is about to erupt—dodge sideways now!
§bAzure Dragon invokes its divine tail: move away!
§bAzure Dragon spreads its wing blades: repeated pursuit slashes!
§cAzure Dragon takes flight: leave the marked impact zone!
§cAzure Dragon lowers its body: clear the warning lane!
§cAzure Dragon lunges to bite: dodge sideways now!
message.sevenstars.azure_dragon_defeated: Seven star marks align: you earned the right to unlock the lost star art
advancement.sevenstars.azure_dragon_defe: Defeat the Azure Dragon and receive the Lost Star Magic Token
